## File: climate-dashboard/static/index.html
````html
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Climate Change — narve.ai</title>
<style>
  :root {
    --bg-0: #0b1220;
    --bg-1: #0f172a;
    --bg-2: #1e293b;
    --border: #243042;
    --text-1: #f1f5f9;
    --text-2: #94a3b8;
    --text-3: #64748b;
    --accent: #34d399;
    --accent-2: #f87171;
    --warn: #fbbf24;
    --good: #34d399;
  }
  * { box-sizing: border-box; }
  body {
    margin: 0;
    background: var(--bg-0);
    color: var(--text-1);
    font: 14px/1.45 -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif;
    -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
  }
  header {
    padding: 18px 24px;
    border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border);
    background: linear-gradient(180deg, var(--bg-1), var(--bg-0));
  }
  header h1 {
    margin: 0;
    font-size: 20px;
    font-weight: 700;
    letter-spacing: -0.01em;
  }
  header .sub {
    margin-top: 3px;
    color: var(--text-2);
    font-size: 12px;
  }
  main { padding: 18px 24px 80px; max-width: 1320px; margin: 0 auto; }
  .grid {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(280px, 1fr));
    gap: 14px;
    margin-bottom: 22px;
  }
  .card {
    background: var(--bg-1);
    border: 1px solid var(--border);
    border-radius: 12px;
    padding: 14px 16px;
  }
  .card h3 {
    margin: 0 0 8px;
    font-size: 11px;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    letter-spacing: 0.06em;
    color: var(--text-3);
    font-weight: 600;
  }
  .card .big {
    font-size: 30px;
    font-weight: 700;
    letter-spacing: -0.02em;
    line-height: 1.1;
  }
  .card .sub {
    color: var(--text-2);
    font-size: 12px;
    margin-top: 4px;
  }
  .pill {
    display: inline-block;
    padding: 2px 8px;
    border-radius: 999px;
    font-size: 11px;
    font-weight: 600;
    background: var(--bg-2);
    color: var(--text-2);
  }
  .pill.hot { background: rgba(248, 113, 113, 0.15); color: var(--accent-2); }
  .pill.warn { background: rgba(251, 191, 36, 0.15); color: var(--warn); }
  .pill.good { background: rgba(52, 211, 153, 0.15); color: var(--good); }

  .regime {
    background: var(--bg-2);
    border: 1px solid var(--border);
    border-radius: 10px;
    padding: 10px 14px;
    margin-bottom: 18px;
    font-size: 12px;
    color: var(--text-2);
    display: flex;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
    gap: 14px;
    align-items: center;
  }
  .regime .label {
    font-weight: 600;
    color: var(--text-3);
    text-transform: uppercase;
    letter-spacing: 0.05em;
    font-size: 10px;
  }

  table {
    width: 100%;
    border-collapse: collapse;
    font-size: 13px;
  }
  th, td {
    text-align: left;
    padding: 9px 12px;
    border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border);
  }
  th {
    color: var(--text-3);
    font-weight: 600;
    font-size: 11px;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    letter-spacing: 0.05em;
    background: var(--bg-1);
    position: sticky;
    top: 0;
  }
  tr:hover td { background: var(--bg-2); }

  .num { text-align: right; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; }
  .edge-pos { color: var(--good); font-weight: 600; }
  .edge-neg { color: var(--accent-2); font-weight: 600; }

  .section-title {
    margin: 28px 0 10px;
    font-size: 13px;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    letter-spacing: 0.06em;
    color: var(--text-3);
    font-weight: 600;
  }

  .spark {
    height: 56px;
    width: 100%;
    margin-top: 10px;
  }
  .empty {
    color: var(--text-3);
    font-style: italic;
    padding: 20px;
    text-align: center;
  }
  a { color: var(--accent); text-decoration: none; }
  a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

  .market-q { font-weight: 500; color: var(--text-1); }
  .market-event { color: var(--text-3); font-size: 11px; }

  .toolbar {
    display: flex;
    gap: 8px;
    margin-bottom: 12px;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
  }
  .toolbar input {
    background: var(--bg-2);
    border: 1px solid var(--border);
    color: var(--text-1);
    padding: 7px 10px;
    border-radius: 8px;
    font-size: 13px;
    flex: 1;
    min-width: 200px;
  }
  .toolbar select {
    background: var(--bg-2);
    border: 1px solid var(--border);
    color: var(--text-1);
    padding: 7px 10px;
    border-radius: 8px;
    font-size: 13px;
  }
  .thresholds {
    display: flex;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
    gap: 5px;
    margin-top: 8px;
  }
  .threshold-pill {
    background: var(--bg-2);
    border: 1px solid var(--border);
    border-radius: 6px;
    padding: 3px 7px;
    font-size: 11px;
    font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums;
    color: var(--text-2);
  }
  .threshold-pill b { color: var(--text-1); }
  .threshold-pill.hot { border-color: rgba(248,113,113,.4); color: var(--accent-2); }
  .threshold-pill.hot b { color: var(--accent-2); }
  .err-zero { color: var(--good); }
  .err-pos { color: var(--accent-2); }
  .err-neg { color: var(--warn); }
</style>
</head>
<body>
<header>
  <h1>Climate Change <span style="font-size:11px;color:var(--text-3);font-weight:500;letter-spacing:.05em;text-transform:uppercase;margin-left:6px">v3</span></h1>
  <div class="sub">Long-horizon climate markets · NASA GISTEMP · NOAA Mauna Loa CO₂ · NOAA GML CH₄ · NSIDC sea ice · OISST · ENSO · backtest</div>
</header>
<main>

<div id="regime" class="regime" style="display:none"></div>

<div class="grid" id="cards"></div>

<div class="section-title">Climate markets</div>
<div class="toolbar">
  <input id="search" placeholder="Search markets…" />
  <select id="sort">
    <option value="edge">Sort: largest edge</option>
    <option value="liquidity">Sort: most liquidity</option>
    <option value="end">Sort: ends soonest</option>
  </select>
</div>
<div id="markets"></div>

<div class="section-title">Model performance — last 5 years</div>
<div class="card" id="backtest" style="padding:0;overflow:auto">
  <div class="empty">Loading backtest…</div>
</div>

<div class="section-title">Sources</div>
<div class="card" style="font-size:12px;color:var(--text-2);line-height:1.7">
  Temperature anomaly: <a href="https://data.giss.nasa.gov/gistemp/" target="_blank">NASA GISTEMP v4</a>
  (baseline 1951–1980).
  CO₂: <a href="https://gml.noaa.gov/ccgg/trends/" target="_blank">NOAA GML Mauna Loa</a>.
  Methane (CH₄): <a href="https://gml.noaa.gov/ccgg/trends_ch4/" target="_blank">NOAA GML globally-averaged CH₄</a>.
  Sea ice: <a href="https://nsidc.org/arcticseaicenews/" target="_blank">NSIDC G02135</a>.
  SST: <a href="https://climatereanalyzer.org/clim/sst_daily/" target="_blank">Climate Reanalyzer / NOAA OISST 2.1</a>.
  ENSO: <a href="https://origin.cpc.ncep.noaa.gov/products/analysis_monitoring/ensostuff/ONI_v5.php" target="_blank">NOAA CPC ONI</a>.
  Markets: <a href="https://gamma-api.polymarket.com" target="_blank">Polymarket Gamma</a>.
</div>

</main>

<script>
const MONTH = ["Jan","Feb","Mar","Apr","May","Jun","Jul","Aug","Sep","Oct","Nov","Dec"];

function fmt(x, dp = 2) {
  if (x === null || x === undefined || Number.isNaN(x)) return "—";
  return Number(x).toLocaleString(undefined, { minimumFractionDigits: dp, maximumFractionDigits: dp });
}
function pct(x) {
  if (x === null || x === undefined) return "—";
  return (x * 100).toFixed(0) + "%";
}

// Tiny inline SVG sparkline
function sparkline(values, w = 260, h = 56, color = "#34d399") {
  if (!values || values.length < 2) return "";
  const min = Math.min(...values), max = Math.max(...values);
  const range = (max - min) || 1;
  const pts = values.map((v, i) => {
    const x = (i / (values.length - 1)) * w;
    const y = h - ((v - min) / range) * h;
    return `${x.toFixed(1)},${y.toFixed(1)}`;
  }).join(" ");
  return `<svg class="spark" viewBox="0 0 ${w} ${h}" preserveAspectRatio="none">
    <polyline fill="none" stroke="${color}" stroke-width="1.5" points="${pts}" />
  </svg>`;
}

async function getJSON(url) {
  try {
    const r = await fetch(url);
    if (!r.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${r.status}`);
    return await r.json();
  } catch (e) {
    console.warn("fetch failed", url, e);
    return null;
  }
}

async function loadRegime() {
  const oni = await getJSON("/api/regime");
  if (!oni || !oni.latest) return;
  const el = document.getElementById("regime");
  el.style.display = "flex";
  const cls = oni.state === "El Niño" ? "hot" : oni.state === "La Niña" ? "good" : "warn";
  el.innerHTML = `
    <span class="label">ENSO</span>
    <span><span class="pill ${cls}">${oni.state}</span> &nbsp; ONI ${fmt(oni.latest.oni, 2)} (${MONTH[oni.latest.month - 1]} ${oni.latest.year})</span>
    <span style="color:var(--text-3)">${oni.source}</span>
  `;
}

async function loadCards() {
  const s = await getJSON("/api/summary");
  const tempData = await getJSON("/api/temperature");
  const co2Data = await getJSON("/api/co2");
  const seaData = await getJSON("/api/sea-ice");
  const ch4Data = await getJSON("/api/methane");

  const cards = [];

  // Temperature anomaly card
  if (s?.gistemp?.projection) {
    const p = s.gistemp.projection;
    const rec = p.current_record;
    const sparkVals = (tempData?.annual || []).slice(-50).map(a => a.anomaly_c);
    const cls = p.p_breaks_record > 0.7 ? "hot" : p.p_breaks_record > 0.4 ? "warn" : "good";
    const thr = s.gistemp.thresholds;
    const threshHtml = thr ? `
      <div class="thresholds">
        ${thr.thresholds.map(t => {
          const hot = t.p_at_or_above >= 0.5;
          return `<span class="threshold-pill ${hot ? 'hot' : ''}">≥${t.threshold_c.toFixed(1)}°C <b>${pct(t.p_at_or_above)}</b></span>`;
        }).join("")}
      </div>` : "";
    cards.push(`
      <div class="card">
        <h3>Global temperature anomaly</h3>
        <div class="big">+${fmt(p.ytd_anomaly_c, 2)}°C</div>
        <div class="sub">${p.months_observed} mo of ${p.current_year} · projects to <b>+${fmt(p.projected_annual_anomaly_c, 2)}°C</b></div>
        <div class="sub" style="margin-top:6px">Record: +${fmt(rec.anomaly_c, 2)}°C (${rec.year})
          &nbsp; <span class="pill ${cls}">P(new record) ${pct(p.p_breaks_record)}</span></div>
        ${threshHtml}
        ${sparkline(sparkVals, 260, 56, "#f87171")}
      </div>
    `);
  }

  // CO2 card
  if (s?.co2?.latest) {
    const l = s.co2.latest;
    const p = s.co2.projection;
    const sparkVals = (co2Data?.monthly || []).slice(-120).map(m => m.ppm);
    const thr = s.co2.thresholds;
    // Show only the most "live" thresholds — those between 5-95% prob
    const liveThr = thr ? thr.thresholds.filter(t => t.p_at_or_above > 0.05 && t.p_at_or_above < 0.95) : [];
    const threshHtml = liveThr.length ? `
      <div class="thresholds">
        ${liveThr.map(t => {
          const hot = t.p_at_or_above >= 0.5;
          return `<span class="threshold-pill ${hot ? 'hot' : ''}">≥${t.threshold_ppm} <b>${pct(t.p_at_or_above)}</b></span>`;
        }).join("")}
      </div>` : "";
    cards.push(`
      <div class="card">
        <h3>Atmospheric CO₂ (Mauna Loa)</h3>
        <div class="big">${fmt(l.ppm, 2)} ppm</div>
        <div class="sub">${MONTH[l.month - 1]} ${l.year} · trend +${fmt(p?.ppm_per_year ?? 0, 2)} ppm/yr · σ=${fmt(p?.residual_std_ppm ?? 0, 2)}</div>
        <div class="sub" style="margin-top:6px">Year-end projection: <b>${fmt(p?.projected_year_end_ppm ?? 0, 2)} ppm</b></div>
        ${threshHtml}
        ${sparkline(sparkVals, 260, 56, "#fbbf24")}
      </div>
    `);
  }

  // Methane card (CH₄)
  if (s?.methane?.latest) {
    const l = s.methane.latest;
    const p = s.methane.projection;
    const sparkVals = (ch4Data?.monthly || []).slice(-120).map(m => m.ppb);
    const thr = s.methane.thresholds;
    const liveThr = thr ? thr.thresholds.filter(t => t.p_at_or_above > 0.05 && t.p_at_or_above < 0.95) : [];
    const threshHtml = liveThr.length ? `
      <div class="thresholds">
        ${liveThr.map(t => {
          const hot = t.p_at_or_above >= 0.5;
          return `<span class="threshold-pill ${hot ? 'hot' : ''}">≥${t.threshold_ppb} <b>${pct(t.p_at_or_above)}</b></span>`;
        }).join("")}
      </div>` : "";
    cards.push(`
      <div class="card">
        <h3>Atmospheric methane (CH₄)</h3>
        <div class="big">${fmt(l.ppb, 0)} <span style="font-size:14px;color:var(--text-2)">ppb</span></div>
        <div class="sub">${MONTH[l.month - 1]} ${l.year} · trend +${fmt(p?.ppb_per_year ?? 0, 1)} ppb/yr · σ=${fmt(p?.residual_std_ppb ?? 0, 1)}</div>
        <div class="sub" style="margin-top:6px">Year-end projection: <b>${fmt(p?.projected_year_end_ppb ?? 0, 1)} ppb</b></div>
        ${threshHtml}
        ${sparkline(sparkVals, 260, 56, "#a78bfa")}
      </div>
    `);
  }

  // Arctic sea ice card
  if (s?.sea_ice?.record_check) {
    const r = s.sea_ice.record_check;
    const ap = s.sea_ice.arctic_projection;
    const sparkVals = (seaData?.arctic_recent || []).slice(-365).map(d => d.extent_mkm2);
    const cls = r.rank_lowest_in_record <= 3 ? "hot" : r.rank_lowest_in_record <= 10 ? "warn" : "good";
    const projHtml = ap ? `<div class="sub" style="margin-top:4px">Summer min projection: <b>${fmt(ap.projected_min_mkm2, 2)} M km²</b> (σ ${fmt(ap.residual_std_mkm2, 2)}, trend ${ap.trend_mkm2_per_year >= 0 ? '+' : ''}${fmt(ap.trend_mkm2_per_year, 3)}/yr)</div>` : "";
    cards.push(`
      <div class="card">
        <h3>Arctic sea ice extent</h3>
        <div class="big">${fmt(r.extent_mkm2, 2)} <span style="font-size:14px;color:var(--text-2)">M km²</span></div>
        <div class="sub">${r.date} · DOY mean ${fmt(r.doy_mean, 2)}, range ${fmt(r.doy_min, 2)}–${fmt(r.doy_max, 2)}</div>
        <div class="sub" style="margin-top:6px">
          <span class="pill ${cls}">#${r.rank_lowest_in_record} lowest of ${r.history_years} on this day</span>
        </div>
        ${projHtml}
        ${sparkline(sparkVals, 260, 56, "#60a5fa")}
      </div>
    `);
  }

  // Antarctic sea ice card
  if (s?.sea_ice?.antarctic_projection && (seaData?.antarctic_recent || []).length) {
    const ap = s.sea_ice.antarctic_projection;
    const recent = seaData.antarctic_recent;
    const latest = recent[recent.length - 1];
    const sparkVals = recent.slice(-365).map(d => d.extent_mkm2);
    cards.push(`
      <div class="card">
        <h3>Antarctic sea ice extent</h3>
        <div class="big">${fmt(latest.extent_mkm2, 2)} <span style="font-size:14px;color:var(--text-2)">M km²</span></div>
        <div class="sub">${latest.year}-${String(latest.month).padStart(2,'0')}-${String(latest.day).padStart(2,'0')}</div>
        <div class="sub" style="margin-top:6px">Summer min projection: <b>${fmt(ap.projected_min_mkm2, 2)} M km²</b> (σ ${fmt(ap.residual_std_mkm2, 2)}, trend ${ap.trend_mkm2_per_year >= 0 ? '+' : ''}${fmt(ap.trend_mkm2_per_year, 3)}/yr)</div>
        ${sparkline(sparkVals, 260, 56, "#a5b4fc")}
      </div>
    `);
  }

  // SST card
  const sst = await getJSON("/api/sst");
  if (sst?.series) {
    const cur = sst.series.find(s => /^\d{4}$/.test(s.name) && Number(s.name) === new Date().getUTCFullYear());
    const mean = sst.series.find(s => /mean/i.test(s.name));
    if (cur?.data) {
      const lastIdx = cur.data.findIndex(v => v === null) - 1;
      const idx = lastIdx > 0 ? lastIdx : cur.data.length - 1;
      const v = cur.data[idx];
      const baseline = mean?.data?.[idx];
      const anom = v && baseline ? v - baseline : null;
      cards.push(`
        <div class="card">
          <h3>Global SST (60°S–60°N)</h3>
          <div class="big">${v ? fmt(v, 2) + "°C" : "—"}</div>
          <div class="sub">DOY ${idx + 1} · 1982-2011 mean ${baseline ? fmt(baseline, 2) + "°C" : "—"}</div>
          ${anom !== null ? `<div class="sub" style="margin-top:6px"><span class="pill ${anom > 0.5 ? 'hot' : 'warn'}">+${fmt(anom, 2)}°C anomaly</span></div>` : ""}
          ${sparkline(cur.data.filter(x => x !== null), 260, 56, "#f87171")}
        </div>
      `);
    }
  }

  document.getElementById("cards").innerHTML = cards.join("");
}

let _allMarkets = [];

function renderMarkets() {
  const q = document.getElementById("search").value.toLowerCase().trim();
  const sortBy = document.getElementById("sort").value;
  let rows = _allMarkets.slice();
  if (q) {
    rows = rows.filter(m => {
      const t = ((m._event_title || "") + " " + (m.question || "")).toLowerCase();
      return t.includes(q);
    });
  }
  rows.sort((a, b) => {
    if (sortBy === "edge") {
      const ea = a._edge_pp === null ? -999 : Math.abs(a._edge_pp);
      const eb = b._edge_pp === null ? -999 : Math.abs(b._edge_pp);
      return eb - ea;
    }
    if (sortBy === "liquidity") {
      return (Number(b.liquidity || 0) - Number(a.liquidity || 0));
    }
    if (sortBy === "end") {
      return new Date(a.endDate || "9999") - new Date(b.endDate || "9999");
    }
    return 0;
  });

  if (!rows.length) {
    document.getElementById("markets").innerHTML = `<div class="empty">No markets match.</div>`;
    return;
  }

  const html = `
    <div class="card" style="padding:0;overflow:auto">
      <table>
        <thead>
          <tr>
            <th>Market</th>
            <th class="num">Implied</th>
            <th class="num">Model</th>
            <th class="num">Edge</th>
            <th class="num">Liquidity</th>
            <th>Ends</th>
          </tr>
        </thead>
        <tbody>
          ${rows.map(m => {
            const slug = m.slug || (m._event_title || "").toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, "-");
            const url = m.slug ? `https://polymarket.com/market/${m.slug}` : null;
            const impl = m._implied_p !== null && m._implied_p !== undefined ? pct(m._implied_p) : "—";
            const mp = m._model_p !== null && m._model_p !== undefined ? pct(m._model_p) : "—";
            const edge = m._edge_pp;
            const edgeHtml = edge === null || edge === undefined ? "—" :
              edge > 0 ? `<span class="edge-pos">+${edge.toFixed(1)} pp</span>` :
                          `<span class="edge-neg">${edge.toFixed(1)} pp</span>`;
            const liq = m.liquidity ? Number(m.liquidity).toLocaleString(undefined, { maximumFractionDigits: 0 }) : "—";
            const end = m.endDate ? new Date(m.endDate).toISOString().slice(0, 10) : "—";
            const q = m.question || "";
            const ev = m._event_title || "";
            const title = url ? `<a href="${url}" target="_blank">${q}</a>` : q;
            const rationale = m._rationale ? `<div class="market-event">${m._rationale}</div>` : "";
            return `<tr>
              <td>
                <div class="market-q">${title}</div>
                <div class="market-event">${ev}</div>
                ${rationale}
              </td>
              <td class="num">${impl}</td>
              <td class="num">${mp}</td>
              <td class="num">${edgeHtml}</td>
              <td class="num">${liq}</td>
              <td>${end}</td>
            </tr>`;
          }).join("")}
        </tbody>
      </table>
    </div>
  `;
  document.getElementById("markets").innerHTML = html;
}

async function loadMarkets() {
  document.getElementById("markets").innerHTML = `<div class="empty">Loading markets…</div>`;
  const r = await getJSON("/api/markets");
  _allMarkets = (r?.markets) || [];
  renderMarkets();
}

async function loadBacktest() {
  const b = await getJSON("/api/backtest");
  const root = document.getElementById("backtest");
  if (!b || (!b.gistemp?.length && !b.co2?.length)) {
    root.innerHTML = `<div class="empty">Backtest unavailable.</div>`;
    return;
  }
  const errCls = (e) => Math.abs(e) < 0.05 ? "err-zero" : e > 0 ? "err-pos" : "err-neg";
  const tempRows = (b.gistemp || []).map(r => `
    <tr>
      <td>Temperature anomaly</td>
      <td class="num">${r.year}</td>
      <td class="num">${fmt(r.projected_c, 2)}°C</td>
      <td class="num">${fmt(r.actual_c, 2)}°C</td>
      <td class="num"><span class="${errCls(r.error_c)}">${r.error_c >= 0 ? '+' : ''}${fmt(r.error_c, 2)}°C</span></td>
    </tr>
  `).join("");
  const co2Rows = (b.co2 || []).map(r => `
    <tr>
      <td>CO₂ year-end ppm</td>
      <td class="num">${r.year}</td>
      <td class="num">${fmt(r.projected_year_end_ppm, 2)}</td>
      <td class="num">${fmt(r.actual_dec_ppm, 2)}</td>
      <td class="num"><span class="${errCls(r.error_ppm / 5)}">${r.error_ppm >= 0 ? '+' : ''}${fmt(r.error_ppm, 2)} ppm</span></td>
    </tr>
  `).join("");
  const ch4Rows = (b.methane || []).map(r => `
    <tr>
      <td>CH₄ year-end ppb</td>
      <td class="num">${r.year}</td>
      <td class="num">${fmt(r.projected_year_end_ppb, 1)}</td>
      <td class="num">${fmt(r.actual_dec_ppb, 1)}</td>
      <td class="num"><span class="${errCls(r.error_ppb / 10)}">${r.error_ppb >= 0 ? '+' : ''}${fmt(r.error_ppb, 1)} ppb</span></td>
    </tr>
  `).join("");
  root.innerHTML = `
    <table>
      <thead>
        <tr>
          <th>Series</th>
          <th class="num">Year</th>
          <th class="num">Projected (Jun)</th>
          <th class="num">Actual</th>
          <th class="num">Error</th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody>${tempRows}${co2Rows}${ch4Rows}</tbody>
    </table>
    <div style="padding:10px 14px;border-top:1px solid var(--border);font-size:11px;color:var(--text-3);line-height:1.5">
      <b>Method:</b> ${b.method?.gistemp || ""} ${b.method?.co2 || ""} ${b.method?.methane || ""}
    </div>
  `;
}

document.getElementById("search").addEventListener("input", renderMarkets);
document.getElementById("sort").addEventListener("change", renderMarkets);

loadRegime();
loadCards();
loadMarkets();
loadBacktest();

// Auto-refresh markets every 5 min
setInterval(loadMarkets, 5 * 60 * 1000);
</script>
</body>
</html>
````

## File: climate-dashboard/README.md
````markdown
# Climate Change Dashboard (v3)

Long-horizon climate prediction markets with model-derived edges.

Listens on `:7052`. Subdomain: `climate.narve.ai` (registered in `gateway/config.json`).

## What's new in v3

- **Atmospheric methane (CH₄)** — adds NOAA GML globally-averaged monthly methane (`ch4_mm_gl.csv`, ppb). Backend follows the same shape as CO₂: 24-month linear regression with residual std, threshold probability table, year-end backtest. New endpoints `/api/methane` and methane block in `/api/summary` + `/api/backtest`. Methane card on the front page, threshold pills (1930 / 1940 / 1950 / … ppb), and a methane row in the model-performance table.
- **Methane market matcher** — handles "above N ppb / below N ppb" plus "above 1.95 ppm" (CH₄ markets are sometimes priced in either unit).

## What was added in v2

- **Antarctic sea-ice min projection** — same 25-year linear-trend / residual-std normal-CDF approach as Arctic; scores antarctic-min markets when they appear.
- **Proper CO₂ threshold model** — the v1 hack returned a flat 85/15. v2 returns the regression's residual std and uses a normal CDF to score "above N ppm" markets.
- **Annual-anomaly threshold model** — P(annual mean ≥ 1.5°C / 1.6°C / 1.7°C) under N(projection, drift_std), exposed as pills in the temperature card and used to score "above N°C" markets.
- **Backtest panel** — replays both models 'as of June' for the last 5 completed years; shown at the bottom of the page and at `/api/backtest`.
- **Broader Polymarket tag coverage** — adds `global-warming`, `sea-level`, `extreme-weather` to the previous three; markets that the dashboard can't model still appear in the table with a blank model column.
- **Tighter market matchers** — handle "below / above / over / under / exceed" + ranges and dropped the over-broad `vs.` reject keyword.

## Data sources

| What | Source | Cadence |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Global temperature anomaly | NASA GISTEMP v4 (`GLB.Ts+dSST.csv`) | monthly |
| Atmospheric CO₂ | NOAA GML Mauna Loa (`co2_mm_mlo.csv`) | monthly |
| Atmospheric CH₄ | NOAA GML globally-averaged (`ch4_mm_gl.csv`) | monthly |
| Arctic + Antarctic sea ice extent | NSIDC Sea Ice Index G02135 v3.0 | daily |
| Global SST 60°S–60°N | NOAA OISST 2.1 via Climate Reanalyzer JSON | daily |
| ENSO state | NOAA CPC Oceanic Niño Index (`oni.data`) | monthly |
| Markets | Polymarket Gamma `tag_slug=climate-change` (+ siblings) | live |

All upstream sources are free and require no API key. Each fetcher has its
own TTL (12h for monthly series, 3–6h for daily, 5min for markets).

## Models

- **Year-end record-pace projection (temperature):** YTD anomaly + average historical drift from same-month-of-year to year-end. P(new record) is a normal CDF using the historical std of that drift.
- **CO₂ trajectory:** linear regression of last 24 months → year-end ppm.
- **Sea ice rank-on-DOY:** today's extent ranked against the same calendar day across the full record.

When a Polymarket market has a discoverable target (e.g. "warmest year on record", "CO₂ above N ppm"), the model probability is attached and an edge in percentage points is computed against the market-implied price.

## Run locally

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
python3 server.py
# → http://localhost:7052
```

## Endpoints

- `GET /api/summary` — single page-load payload: temperature + CO₂ + ice projections, threshold probabilities, ENSO state
- `GET /api/markets` — climate markets with model edges (Arctic + Antarctic sea ice, year-end record, anomaly threshold, CO₂ threshold)
- `GET /api/temperature` — full GISTEMP series + projection
- `GET /api/co2` — full Mauna Loa series + projection (with residual std)
- `GET /api/methane` — full NOAA GML CH₄ series + projection + threshold probabilities
- `GET /api/sea-ice` — Arctic + Antarctic recent series + record check
- `GET /api/sst` — Climate Reanalyzer JSON (whole multi-year structure)
- `GET /api/regime` — ONI + ENSO state
- `GET /api/backtest` — last 5 completed years, projection-vs-actual for both temperature and CO₂ models
- `GET /api/health` — liveness

## Container

```bash
docker build -t climate-dashboard .
docker run --rm -p 7052:7052 climate-dashboard
```

## Notes

- This dashboard intentionally does **not** cover natural disasters or short-term weather — those live in `polymarket_weather_dashboard` and (planned) the Major Disasters dashboard.
- The Polymarket fetcher reuses the same gamma pattern as the weather dashboard, but applies a stricter climate-keyword filter so it doesn't sweep in tornado/hurricane markets.
- ENSO state (ONI) is shown as a banner because it's the single most useful short-term covariate for both temperature and SST markets.
````

## File: climate-dashboard/server.py
````python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Polymarket Climate Change Dashboard — Flask backend (v3).

Long-horizon climate markets: warmest year on record, Arctic + Antarctic sea
ice extent, global mean temperature anomaly, atmospheric CO2 + CH4, sea
surface temperature, ENSO regime.

Data sources:
  - NASA GISTEMP v4 (monthly global land+ocean anomaly vs 1951-1980 baseline)
  - NOAA GML Mauna Loa atmospheric CO2 (monthly mean)
  - NOAA GML globally-averaged methane CH4 (monthly mean)        [v3]
  - NSIDC Sea Ice Index G02135 v4.0 (daily Arctic + Antarctic extent)
  - Climate Reanalyzer / NOAA OISST (daily global SST)
  - NOAA CPC ONI (Oceanic Nino Index — ENSO state)

Markets via Polymarket Gamma API. Tag slugs scanned: climate-change,
global-temperature, climate, global-warming, sea-level, extreme-weather.

Models (v2):
  - Year-end record-pace projection from YTD anomaly + historical drift,
    plus N(μ, σ) threshold probabilities (P(annual ≥ 1.5 / 1.6 / 1.7°C)).
  - 24-month linear regression on Mauna Loa CO2 with residual-std threshold
    probabilities (P(year-end ≥ 425 / 430 ppm), etc.).
  - 25-year linear-trend projections of Arctic AND Antarctic annual minimum
    sea ice extent, with normal-CDF probability bands for binned markets.
  - Backtest: each model is replayed 'as of June' for the last 5 completed
    years and reported as projected-vs-actual at /api/backtest.

Edges = (model_p − implied_p) in percentage points.
"""
⋮----
logger = logging.getLogger("climate")
⋮----
app = Flask(__name__, static_folder="static")
⋮----
# Gzip compression
⋮----
PORT = int(os.environ.get("PORT", "7052"))
⋮----
# ─── Cache ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
⋮----
_cache: "OrderedDict[str, dict]" = OrderedDict()
_cache_lock = threading.Lock()
⋮----
# Per-key TTL overrides (seconds). Climate data updates monthly/daily so we cache
# generously — re-pulling NASA CSVs every minute would be wasteful and rude.
_TTL_DEFAULT = 60 * 60  # 1h
_TTL: dict[str, int] = {
⋮----
"gistemp": 60 * 60 * 12,        # GISTEMP updates monthly, refresh twice a day
"co2": 60 * 60 * 12,            # NOAA Mauna Loa updates monthly
"methane": 60 * 60 * 12,        # NOAA GML CH4 updates monthly
"sea_ice": 60 * 60 * 6,         # NSIDC updates daily
"sst": 60 * 60 * 3,             # OISST daily
"oni": 60 * 60 * 12,            # CPC ONI updates monthly
"polymarket": 60 * 5,            # Markets move — refresh every 5 min
⋮----
def cache_get(key: str)
⋮----
entry = _cache.get(key)
⋮----
ttl = _TTL.get(key, _TTL_DEFAULT)
⋮----
def cache_set(key: str, data) -> None
⋮----
# Cap cache size
⋮----
# ─── HTTP helper ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
⋮----
_USER_AGENT = "polymarket-climate-dashboard/1.0 (+https://climate.narve.ai)"
⋮----
def _http_get(url: str, *, timeout: int = 20, params: Optional[dict] = None) -> Optional[requests.Response]
⋮----
r = requests.get(url, params=params, timeout=timeout, headers={"User-Agent": _USER_AGENT})
⋮----
# ─── Polymarket gamma fetcher ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
⋮----
GAMMA_BASE = "https://gamma-api.polymarket.com"
⋮----
CLIMATE_TAG_SLUGS = [
⋮----
# Reject sports / politics / crypto markets that share keywords with climate.
# Note: do NOT use "vs." here — climate markets sometimes phrase comparisons
# (e.g. "Arctic vs Antarctic") with that token.
REJECT_KEYWORDS = [
⋮----
CLIMATE_KEYWORDS = [
⋮----

⋮----
offset = 0
for _ in range(8):  # cap pagination
r = _http_get(
⋮----

⋮----

GISTEMP_URL = "https://data.giss.nasa.gov/gistemp/tabledata_v4/GLB.Ts+dSST.csv"
⋮----
def fetch_gistemp() -> Optional[dict]
⋮----

ann = parts[13].strip()
⋮----
out = {
⋮----
# ─── NOAA Mauna Loa CO2 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def methane_year_end_projection(ch4: dict) -> Optional[dict]
⋮----
"""24-month linear regression on globally-averaged CH4, evaluated at year-end."""
⋮----
series = ch4["monthly"]
cur_year = max(s["year"] for s in series)
tail = series[-24:]
⋮----
xs = [s["decimal_date"] for s in tail]
ys = [s["ppb"] for s in tail]
n = len(xs)
mx = sum(xs) / n
my = sum(ys) / n
num = sum((xs[i] - mx) * (ys[i] - my) for i in range(n))
den = sum((xs[i] - mx) ** 2 for i in range(n))
⋮----
slope = num / den  # ppb per year
intercept = my - slope * mx
projected = intercept + slope * (cur_year + 1.0)
resid = [ys[i] - (intercept + slope * xs[i]) for i in range(n)]
sigma = math.sqrt(sum(r * r for r in resid) / n) if n > 0 else 5.0
sigma = max(sigma, 2.0)  # CH4 is noisier than CO2 — minimum 2 ppb σ
⋮----
"""P(year-end CH4 ≥ T) under N(projection, residual_std)."""
⋮----
mu = ch4_proj.get("projected_year_end_ppb")
sigma = ch4_proj.get("residual_std_ppb") or 5.0
⋮----
out = []
⋮----
p = 1.0 - _normal_cdf((t - mu) / sigma)
⋮----

def annual_record_pace_projection(gistemp: dict) -> Optional[dict]
⋮----
"""Project this year's annual mean using YTD progress and historical analog years."""
⋮----
monthly = gistemp["monthly"]
annual = gistemp["annual"]
⋮----
cur_year = max(m["year"] for m in monthly)
cur_months = sorted([m for m in monthly if m["year"] == cur_year], key=lambda x: x["month"])
⋮----
n = len(cur_months)
ytd_mean = sum(m["anomaly_c"] for m in cur_months) / n
⋮----
# For each historical year, compute (YTD-through-month-n mean, annual mean)
diffs = []
⋮----
ms = sorted([m for m in monthly if m["year"] == y], key=lambda x: x["month"])
⋮----
ytd_y = sum(m["anomaly_c"] for m in ms[:n]) / n
ann_y = sum(m["anomaly_c"] for m in ms) / 12
⋮----
drift = sum(diffs) / len(diffs)
drift_std = math.sqrt(sum((d - drift) ** 2 for d in diffs) / len(diffs)) if len(diffs) > 1 else 0.05
projection = round(ytd_mean + drift, 3)
⋮----
# Record so far
record = max(annual, key=lambda a: a["anomaly_c"])
p_breaks_record = _normal_cdf((projection - record["anomaly_c"]) / max(drift_std, 0.01))
⋮----

⋮----
# Abramowitz & Stegun approximation
⋮----
def co2_year_end_projection(co2: dict) -> Optional[dict]
⋮----
series = co2["monthly"]
⋮----
# Linear regression on last 24 months
⋮----
ys = [s["ppm"] for s in tail]
⋮----
slope = num / den  # ppm per year
⋮----
year_end_decimal = cur_year + 1.0  # i.e. Jan 1 next year ≈ year-end
projected = intercept + slope * year_end_decimal
# Residual std around the trend line (in-sample). Inflate slightly to cover
# forecast horizon out to year-end.
⋮----
sigma = math.sqrt(sum(r * r for r in resid) / n) if n > 0 else 0.5
sigma = max(sigma, 0.3)  # floor — Mauna Loa is too clean to claim < 0.3 ppm
⋮----
def sea_ice_record_check(sea_ice: dict) -> Optional[dict]

⋮----

rank = 1 + sum(1 for e in extents if e < latest["extent_mkm2"])
⋮----
def arctic_min_projection(sea_ice: dict) -> Optional[dict]
⋮----
"""Project this summer's Arctic minimum extent from the historical trend.

    Each year's annual minimum (typically mid-September) is treated as the
    target. We linear-regress the past 25 years' minima against year, and
    quantify the residual std for probability bands.
    """
⋮----
# Group by year, take min of each
by_year: dict[int, float] = {}
by_year_doy: dict[int, tuple[int, int]] = {}
⋮----
y = s["year"]
e = s["extent_mkm2"]
⋮----
cur_year = max(by_year.keys())
# If current year hasn't reached its min yet (typical until late Sept),
# exclude it from the regression so we project FOR it.
cur_doy = by_year_doy[cur_year]
is_post_min = cur_doy[0] >= 9 and cur_doy[1] >= 15
fit_years = sorted(y for y in by_year if y != cur_year or is_post_min)
fit_years = [y for y in fit_years if y >= cur_year - 25]  # last 25y window
xs = [float(y) for y in fit_years]
ys = [by_year[y] for y in fit_years]
⋮----
# residuals
⋮----
sigma = math.sqrt(sum(r * r for r in resid) / n) if n > 0 else 0.3
projected = intercept + slope * cur_year
⋮----
def antarctic_min_projection(sea_ice: dict) -> Optional[dict]
⋮----
"""Same trick as ``arctic_min_projection`` but for the southern-hemisphere
    annual minimum (typically late February / early March)."""
⋮----
series = sea_ice["antarctic"]
⋮----
# Antarctic min is typically Feb 14 – early March. After ~Mar 15 we treat
# the year's minimum as found.
is_post_min = cur_doy[0] > 3 or (cur_doy[0] == 3 and cur_doy[1] >= 15)
⋮----
fit_years = [y for y in fit_years if y >= cur_year - 25]
⋮----
sigma = math.sqrt(sum(r * r for r in resid) / n) if n > 0 else 0.4
⋮----
"""For each anomaly threshold, P(annual mean ≥ T) under N(projection, drift_std)."""
⋮----

sigma = gistemp_proj.get("drift_std_c") or 0.05
⋮----
sigma = max(sigma, 0.03)
⋮----
"""P(year-end ppm ≥ T) under normal centered on regression projection."""
⋮----
mu = co2_proj.get("projected_year_end_ppm")
sigma = co2_proj.get("residual_std_ppm") or 0.5
⋮----
def gistemp_backtest(gistemp: Optional[dict], n_years: int = 5) -> list[dict]
⋮----
"""For the last ``n_years`` completed years, what would the year-end model

    the actual annual mean."""

# "As of June" projection: feed the model the first 6 months of target_year
# and the entire history up to (but not including) target_year.
as_of = 6
ytd = sum(m["anomaly_c"] for m in yr_months[:as_of]) / as_of
# Historical drift from June-YTD to year-end across years strictly before target_year

⋮----
projected = round(ytd + drift, 3)

def co2_backtest(co2: Optional[dict], n_years: int = 5) -> list[dict]
⋮----
"""For each of the last ``n_years`` completed years, fit the same 24-month

⋮----
# "Mid-year" reference: latest available June reading of target_year

_RE_LT = re.compile(r"(?:less than|below|under)\s*([\d.]+)\s*m", re.I)
_RE_GE = re.compile(r"(?:at least|more than|above|over|exceed[a-z]*)\s*([\d.]+)\s*m", re.I)
_RE_BETWEEN = re.compile(r"between\s*([\d.]+)\s*m?\s*(?:&|and|to|-)\s*([\d.]+)\s*m", re.I)

_RE_ANOMALY_LT = re.compile(r"(?:below|under|less than)\s*\+?\s*([\d.]+)\s*°?\s*c", re.I)
````

## File: crypto-dashboard/news_trade_scanner.py
````python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
News-Trade Correlation Scanner (v2)

LOGIC: Trades first, news second.

1. Pull flagged suspicious trades from the existing scanner (all odds, not
   just long shots — a $50K bet at 40% odds is just as suspicious if news
   validates it 2 hours later).
2. Extract the market topics from those trades (e.g. "Iran", "Trump",
   "Fed rate", "Bitcoin").
3. Scan breaking news for events that MATCH those market topics.
4. When a news event correlates with a previously flagged trade, score the
   correlation: how big was the bet, how soon before the news, how much
   would the bettor profit.
5. These are the real insider-trade alerts.

Runs every 20 minutes from the server background task.
"""
⋮----
# ─── Config ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CACHE_DIR = Path(__file__).parent / "cache"
⋮----
# RSS feeds for breaking news
NEWS_FEEDS = [
⋮----
# Common words to ignore when matching trade topics to news
STOP_WORDS = frozenset({
⋮----
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 1: EXTRACT TOPICS FROM SUSPICIOUS TRADES
⋮----
def _stem(word: str) -> str
⋮----
"""Crude suffix stripping so 'attacked' matches 'attack', etc."""
w = word.lower()
⋮----
def extract_trade_topics(suspicious_trades: list[dict]) -> dict[str, list[dict]]
⋮----
"""Extract searchable topic keywords from each suspicious trade's market title.

    Returns: {keyword: [list of trades mentioning that keyword]}

    Uses stemming so "attacked" in a trade title can match "attack" or
    "airstrikes" won't match but "attack" will match "attacked"/"attacks".
    Also extracts named entities (proper nouns) which are the strongest signal.
    """
topic_map: dict[str, list[dict]] = defaultdict(list)
⋮----
title = trade.get("title", "")
⋮----
# Extract meaningful words (3+ chars, not stop words)
raw_words = set(re.findall(r'\b[a-zA-Z]{3,}\b', title.lower()))
⋮----
# Add both original and stemmed forms
words = set()
⋮----
stemmed = _stem(w)
⋮----
# Extract named entities: capitalized words (country names, people, orgs)
entities = re.findall(r'\b[A-Z][a-z]{2,}(?:\s+[A-Z][a-z]{2,})*\b', title)
⋮----
key = ent.lower()
⋮----
# STEP 2: FETCH BREAKING NEWS
⋮----
def _strip_html(text: str) -> str
⋮----
def _parse_rss_items(xml_content: bytes, source: str) -> list[dict]
⋮----
"""Parse RSS/Atom XML into article dicts."""
articles = []
⋮----
root = ET.fromstring(xml_content)
# RSS 2.0
⋮----
title = (item.findtext("title") or "").strip()
link = (item.findtext("link") or "").strip()
pub = (item.findtext("pubDate") or "").strip()
desc = (item.findtext("description") or "").strip()
⋮----
# Atom fallback
⋮----
ns = {"atom": "http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom"}
⋮----
title = (entry.findtext("atom:title", namespaces=ns) or "").strip()
link_el = entry.find("atom:link", ns)
link = link_el.get("href", "") if link_el is not None else ""
pub = (entry.findtext("atom:published", namespaces=ns)
desc = (entry.findtext("atom:summary", namespaces=ns) or "").strip()
⋮----
def fetch_breaking_news() -> list[dict]
⋮----
"""Fetch recent articles from all RSS feeds."""

"""Score how suspicious a trade-news correlation is."""

⋮----

score += 5   # medium odds — suspicious if large + news confirms
⋮----
score += 3   # slight favorite — still flag if correlated
# Even 70%+ odds: if someone drops $200K right before news, it's notable
⋮----
# 5. Original suspicion score from the trade scanner
trade_score = trade.get("score", 0)
````

## File: crypto-dashboard/progress.html
````html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="UTF-8">
<meta http-equiv="refresh" content="30">
<title>Pipeline Progress</title>
<link rel="icon" type="image/png" href="favicon.png">
<style>
  :root { --bg:#0d1117; --card:#161b22; --border:#30363d; --text:#e6edf3; --muted:#8b949e;
          --green:#3fb950; --red:#f85149; --blue:#58a6ff; --yellow:#d29922; }
  * { margin:0; padding:0; box-sizing:border-box; }
  body { background:var(--bg); color:var(--text); font-family:-apple-system,'Segoe UI',sans-serif;
         padding:40px; display:flex; flex-direction:column; align-items:center; min-height:100vh; }
  h1 { font-size:1.5em; margin-bottom:24px; }
  .pipeline { width:100%; max-width:700px; }
  .asset-row { background:var(--card); border:1px solid var(--border); border-radius:10px;
               padding:20px; margin-bottom:14px; }
  .asset-header { display:flex; justify-content:space-between; align-items:center; margin-bottom:10px; }
  .asset-name { font-size:1.2em; font-weight:700; }
  .status-badge { font-size:0.75em; font-weight:600; padding:4px 10px; border-radius:12px; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.05em; }
  .status-done { background:rgba(63,185,80,0.15); color:var(--green); }
  .status-active { background:rgba(88,166,255,0.15); color:var(--blue); }
  .status-pending { background:rgba(139,148,158,0.15); color:var(--muted); }
  .bar-bg { width:100%; height:8px; background:var(--border); border-radius:4px; overflow:hidden; margin-bottom:8px; }
  .bar-fill { height:100%; border-radius:4px; transition:width 0.5s ease; }
  .bar-done { background:var(--green); }
  .bar-active { background:var(--blue); animation:pulse 1.5s infinite; }
  .bar-pending { background:var(--muted); }
  .details { color:var(--muted); font-size:0.8em; display:flex; justify-content:space-between; }
  .accuracy { color:var(--green); font-weight:600; }
  .timeline { width:100%; max-width:700px; margin-top:20px; background:var(--card); border:1px solid var(--border);
              border-radius:10px; padding:20px; }
  .timeline h3 { font-size:0.9em; color:var(--muted); margin-bottom:12px; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.05em; }
  .est-row { display:flex; justify-content:space-between; padding:6px 0; border-bottom:1px solid var(--border); font-size:0.85em; }
  .est-row:last-child { border-bottom:none; }
  @keyframes pulse { 0%,100%{opacity:1;} 50%{opacity:0.5;} }
  .total-bar { margin-top:20px; width:100%; max-width:700px; }
  .total-label { display:flex; justify-content:space-between; font-size:0.85em; color:var(--muted); margin-bottom:6px; }
  .total-bar-bg { width:100%; height:12px; background:var(--border); border-radius:6px; overflow:hidden; }
  .total-bar-fill { height:100%; background:linear-gradient(90deg, var(--green), var(--blue)); border-radius:6px; transition:width 0.5s; }
</style>
</head>
<body>

<h1>Multi-Asset Pipeline Progress</h1>

<div class="pipeline" id="pipeline"></div>

<div class="total-bar">
  <div class="total-label"><span>Overall Progress</span><span id="total-pct">—</span></div>
  <div class="total-bar-bg"><div class="total-bar-fill" id="total-fill" style="width:0%"></div></div>
</div>

<div class="timeline">
  <h3>Estimated Timeline</h3>
  <div id="timeline"></div>
</div>

<script>
const ASSETS = [
  { ticker: "BTC",  name: "Bitcoin",  steps: ["fetch","train","backtest","predict"] },
  { ticker: "ETH",  name: "Ethereum", steps: ["fetch","train","backtest","predict"] },
  { ticker: "SOL",  name: "Solana",   steps: ["fetch","train","backtest","predict"] },
  { ticker: "DOGE", name: "Dogecoin", steps: ["fetch","train","backtest","predict"] },
  { ticker: "XRP",  name: "XRP",      steps: ["fetch","train","backtest","predict"] },
];

// Hardcoded current state — this page auto-refreshes every 30s
// Update these based on actual progress from the log
const PROGRESS = {
  BTC:  { status: "done",    pct: 100, accuracy: "54.8%", hc: "55.1%", detail: "2.6M candles, 7 models trained" },
  ETH:  { status: "done",    pct: 100, accuracy: "51.1%", hc: "52.2%", detail: "2.6M candles, 7 models trained" },
  SOL:  { status: "done",    pct: 100, accuracy: "51.0%", hc: "52.0%", detail: "2.6M candles, 7 models trained" },
  DOGE: { status: "done",    pct: 100, accuracy: "52.3%", hc: "51.4%", detail: "2.6M candles, 7 models trained" },
  XRP:  { status: "active",  pct: 10,  accuracy: "—",     hc: "—",     detail: "Fetching data... ~10%" },
};

// Per-asset time estimates (minutes)
const TIME_PER_ASSET = 15; // ~8 min fetch + ~5 min train + ~2 min backtest
const START_TIME = new Date("2026-03-30T15:40:00Z"); // approximate start

const pipeline = document.getElementById("pipeline");
const timeline = document.getElementById("timeline");

let totalPct = 0;
let html = "";
let timeHtml = "";

ASSETS.forEach((asset, i) => {
  const p = PROGRESS[asset.ticker];
  const statusClass = p.status === "done" ? "status-done" : (p.status === "active" ? "status-active" : "status-pending");
  const barClass = p.status === "done" ? "bar-done" : (p.status === "active" ? "bar-active" : "bar-pending");
  const statusText = p.status === "done" ? "Complete" : (p.status === "active" ? "In Progress" : "Pending");

  totalPct += p.pct;

  const accHtml = p.accuracy !== "—" ? `<span class="accuracy">Acc: ${p.accuracy} | HC: ${p.hc}</span>` : "";

  html += `
    <div class="asset-row">
      <div class="asset-header">
        <span class="asset-name">${asset.ticker} — ${asset.name}</span>
        <span class="status-badge ${statusClass}">${statusText}</span>
      </div>
      <div class="bar-bg"><div class="bar-fill ${barClass}" style="width:${p.pct}%"></div></div>
      <div class="details">
        <span>${p.detail}</span>
        ${accHtml}
      </div>
    </div>`;

  // Timeline estimate
  const estStart = new Date(START_TIME.getTime() + i * TIME_PER_ASSET * 60000);
  const estEnd = new Date(estStart.getTime() + TIME_PER_ASSET * 60000);
  const now = new Date();
  let timeStatus = "";
  if (p.status === "done") timeStatus = "&#10003; Done";
  else if (p.status === "active") timeStatus = `<span style="color:var(--blue)">~${Math.max(0, Math.round((estEnd - now)/60000))} min left</span>`;
  else timeStatus = `<span style="color:var(--muted)">ETA: ${estEnd.toLocaleTimeString([], {hour:'2-digit',minute:'2-digit'})}</span>`;

  timeHtml += `
    <div class="est-row">
      <span>${asset.ticker}</span>
      <span>${estStart.toLocaleTimeString([], {hour:'2-digit',minute:'2-digit'})} → ${estEnd.toLocaleTimeString([], {hour:'2-digit',minute:'2-digit'})}</span>
      <span>${timeStatus}</span>
    </div>`;
});

pipeline.innerHTML = html;
timeline.innerHTML = timeHtml;

const overallPct = Math.round(totalPct / ASSETS.length);
document.getElementById("total-pct").textContent = overallPct + "%";
document.getElementById("total-fill").style.width = overallPct + "%";
</script>

</body>
</html>
````

## File: Dashboard-x-truth-research-prediction/.env.example
````
# X (Twitter) — Basic tier required ($100/month), Bearer Token only needed
TWITTER_BEARER_TOKEN=
TWITTER_MONTHLY_QUOTA=10000

# TruthSocial — free, no API key needed for public accounts
TRUTHSOCIAL_USERNAME=
TRUTHSOCIAL_PASSWORD=
TRUTHSOCIAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=
TRUTHSOCIAL_API_BASE_URL=https://truthsocial.com

# Dashboard Auth
DASHBOARD_USER=admin
# REQUIRED: Set a strong password. The app will refuse to start without one.
DASHBOARD_PASSWORD=

# App
DATABASE_URL=sqlite+aiosqlite:///./predictions.db
LOG_LEVEL=INFO
````

## File: Dashboard-x-truth-research-prediction/README.md
````markdown
# Polymarket Prediction Intelligence Dashboard

Scrapes X (Twitter) and TruthSocial for prediction-related posts, cross-references against live Polymarket odds, scores by EV and source credibility, surfaces the best betting opportunities.

## Quick Start

```bash
cd Dashboard-x-truth-research-prediction
cp .env.example .env   # edit with your credentials
docker-compose up --build
# Open http://127.0.0.1:18789
# Login: admin / changeme
```

## Without Docker

```bash
python3 -m venv venv && source venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
uvicorn app.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 18789
```

## macOS Desktop App

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
bash scripts/build_dmg.sh
# Output: PolymarketDashboard-1.0.0-arm64.dmg
```

Requires: macOS 13+, Apple Silicon, `brew install create-dmg`

## Tests

```bash
pytest app/tests/ -v
```

## Configuration

Edit `app/config.yaml` for keywords, credibility weights, risk thresholds, quota limits.
Edit `.env` for API credentials and auth.

## Troubleshooting

| Problem | Solution |
|---------|----------|
| "Auth required" JSON | Clear cookies, go to /login |
| No predictions | Add API creds to .env, click Refresh |
| Markets not matching | Lower market_match_threshold in config.yaml |
| X quota exhausted | Resets 1st of month |
| "App is damaged" (macOS) | Right-click -> Open |

## Files in this directory

**Application code**
| File / dir | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `app/` | Python application package — FastAPI app, scraping pipeline, scoring, desktop wrapper. See `app/README.md`. |
| `scripts/` | Build/release helpers — DMG builder, icon generator. See `scripts/README.md`. |

**Config / data**
| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `polymarket.spec` | PyInstaller spec file. Used by `scripts/build_dmg.sh` to bundle the desktop app. |
| `predictions.db` (+ `-shm`, `-wal`) | Main SQLite DB. Stores raw posts, predictions, sources, market snapshots, credibility history. |
| `.encryption_key` | App-managed encryption key for sensitive fields. Auto-generated, never commit. |

**Docker / build**
| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `Dockerfile` | Container build for the `truth-research` service. |
| `docker-compose.yml` | **Standalone** compose file (separate from the root one). Brings up just this dashboard. |
| `.dockerignore` | Excludes `*.db`, `.env`, `dist/`, etc. from the Docker build context. |
| `requirements.txt` | Python deps (FastAPI, SQLModel, APScheduler, httpx, tweepy, etc.). |
| `.env` / `.env.example` | Per-service env vars. Copy `.env.example` to `.env` to use. |
| `README.md` | This file. |
````

````html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>betyc — staging</title>
  <link rel="icon" type="image/png" href="/_gateway_static/favicon.png">
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="/_gateway_static/gateway.css">
  <style>
    body {
      margin: 0;
      min-height: 100vh;
      display: flex;
      flex-direction: column;
      align-items: center;
      justify-content: center;
      background: var(--bg-primary, #0a0b14);
      color: var(--text-primary, #e2e4ea);
      font-family: 'Inter', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif;
    }
    .staging-banner {
      position: fixed;
      top: 0;
      left: 0;
      right: 0;
      background: #f59e0b;
      color: #000;
      text-align: center;
      padding: 6px 12px;
      font-size: 13px;
      font-weight: 600;
      letter-spacing: 0.03em;
      z-index: 9999;
    }
    .teaser {
      text-align: center;
      padding: 32px;
    }
    .teaser-logo {
      width: 64px;
      height: 64px;
      border-radius: 16px;
      background: #1a1e2e;
      display: flex;
      align-items: center;
      justify-content: center;
      margin: 0 auto 40px;
    }
    .teaser-logo svg {
      width: 28px;
      height: 28px;
      fill: #e2e2e8;
    }
    .teaser h1 {
      font-size: clamp(28px, 5vw, 48px);
      font-weight: 700;
      letter-spacing: -0.03em;
      margin: 0 0 16px;
      color: var(--text-primary, #e2e4ea);
    }
    .teaser p {
      font-size: 16px;
      color: var(--text-muted, #5a5e72);
      margin: 0 0 40px;
    }
    .teaser-login {
      display: inline-block;
      font-size: 14px;
      font-weight: 500;
      color: var(--accent, #6366f1);
      text-decoration: none;
      border: 1px solid var(--accent, #6366f1);
      padding: 10px 24px;
      border-radius: 8px;
      transition: background 0.15s;
    }
    .teaser-login:hover {
      background: rgba(99, 102, 241, 0.08);
    }
  </style>
</head>
<body class="landing-body">
  <div class="staging-banner">STAGING — edit files in gateway/static/dummy/</div>
  <div class="teaser">
    <div class="teaser-logo"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="#e2e2e8"><polygon points="8,5 18,12 8,19"/></svg></div>
    <h1>Something is coming.</h1>
    <p>Stay tuned.</p>
    <a href="/gate" class="teaser-login">Client Login</a>
  </div>
</body>
</html>
````

## File: gateway/static/sse-client.js
````javascript
/**
 * SSE client for real-time dashboard updates.
 *
 * Injected by the gateway into every proxied HTML response.
 * Connects to /api/stream and refreshes dashboard data when the
 * backend publishes a data_updated event through Redis.
 *
 * How it works:
 *   1. Opens an EventSource to the gateway SSE endpoint.
 *   2. On "data_updated" → finds all elements with [data-sse-refresh]
 *      and re-fetches their data-src URL, or simply re-fetches the
 *      page's main /api/* endpoint and dispatches a custom event.
 *   3. Falls back to periodic polling if SSE is unavailable.
 */
⋮----
// Which dashboard are we on? Extracted from the subdomain.
⋮----
if (!dashboard) return; // apex domain, no SSE needed
⋮----
// ── Indicator badge (top-right corner) ──────────────────────────────
⋮----
function setBadge(text, color)
⋮----
// ── Connect to SSE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
function connect()
⋮----
// Flash the badge
⋮----
// Dispatch a custom event so dashboard JS can react.
⋮----
// Auto-refresh: re-fetch any element with data-sse-refresh attribute.
⋮----
// Cache was just warmed by the background poller — same as data_updated.
⋮----
// Connection still alive, nothing to do.
⋮----
// ── Auto-refresh logic ──────────────────────────────────────────────
⋮----
function refreshTaggedElements()
⋮----
// Elements like <div data-sse-refresh="/api/data"> get their
// innerHTML replaced with a fresh fetch.
⋮----
// For SPAs / JS-heavy dashboards: just refetch the main API and let
// the dashboard's own JS handle the update via the custom event.
// This is the most common pattern — dashboards listen to
// window.addEventListener("dashboard:data_updated", handler).
⋮----
// ── Fallback polling (if SSE dies) ──────────────────────────────────
⋮----
function startFallbackPoll()
⋮----
function clearFallbackPoll()
⋮----
// ── Start ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
````

## File: gateway/cache.py

````python
"""
Redis caching layer for the gateway.

Provides:
- Key-value caching with TTL for API responses
- Pub/sub for real-time data-change notifications (drives SSE)
- Dashboard-specific cache namespacing

Usage from gateway:
    from cache import cache
    cache.get_api("sports", "/api/data")
    cache.set_api("sports", "/api/data", json_bytes, ttl=30)
    cache.publish("sports", "data_updated")

Usage from dashboard backends (optional — publish when fresh data arrives):
    import redis
    r = redis.Redis()
    r.publish("dashboard:sports", json.dumps({"event": "data_updated"}))
"""

# faster data get shorter TTLs.
DEFAULT_TTLS: dict[str, int] = {
⋮----
FALLBACK_TTL = 30  # seconds
⋮----

safe_url = re.sub(r"://[^@]+@", "://****@", self._url)
````

## File: gateway/poller.py
````python
"""
Background data poller for the gateway.

Periodically fetches each dashboard's main API endpoints, stores the
responses in Redis, and publishes data_updated events so SSE clients
refresh instantly.

This means:
  - First page load is always instant (served from cache)
  - Data is never more than one poll interval stale
  - SSE clients get pushed the moment fresh data lands
"""
⋮----

⋮----

# endpoint: the path to poll on the local backend
# interval: seconds between polls (should be < the cache TTL)

POLL_TARGETS: dict[str, list[dict]] = {
⋮----

⋮----

⋮----
"""dashboards: gateway config.json['dashboards'] dict."""

async def start(self) -> None
⋮----
"""Launch one async task per (dashboard, endpoint) pair."""
⋮----
cfg = self._dashboards.get(dash_key)
⋮----
port = cfg["target"]
⋮----
task = asyncio.create_task(
⋮----
async def stop(self) -> None
⋮----
"""Cancel all polling tasks."""
⋮----
async def _poll_loop(self, dashboard: str, port: int, endpoint: str, interval: int) -> None
⋮----
"""Repeatedly fetch one endpoint and cache the result."""
url = f"http://127.0.0.1:{port}{endpoint}"
ttl = DEFAULT_TTLS.get(dashboard, 30)
# Cache TTL should be longer than poll interval so there's always
# a warm entry between polls.
cache_ttl = max(ttl, interval + 10)
⋮----
t0 = time.monotonic()
resp = await self._client.get(url)
elapsed = time.monotonic() - t0
⋮----
content_type = resp.headers.get("content-type", "application/json")
⋮----
def stats(self) -> dict
⋮----
"""Return poller status for the admin panel."""
````

## File: gateway/sse.py
````python
"""
Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream for real-time dashboard updates.

The gateway subscribes to Redis pub/sub channels (one per dashboard).
Connected browsers receive events like:

    event: data_updated
    data: {"dashboard":"sports","ts":1712500000.0}

Frontend JS listens on EventSource("/api/stream?dashboards=sports,crypto")
and triggers a data refresh when it receives an event for its dashboard.
"""
⋮----
log = logging.getLogger("gateway.sse")
⋮----
# Track connected clients for the admin stats endpoint.
_active_connections = 0
⋮----
def active_connection_count() -> int
⋮----
"""Yield SSE-formatted strings. One generator per connected client.

    Args:
        dashboards: Which dashboard channels to subscribe to.
        heartbeat_interval: Seconds between keep-alive pings.
    """
⋮----
pubsub = cache.pubsub()
⋮----
# Redis unavailable — send an error event then close.
⋮----
channels = [f"dashboard:{d}" for d in dashboards]
⋮----
# Initial connection event so the client knows the stream is live.
⋮----
last_heartbeat = time.time()
⋮----
# Non-blocking poll — check for a message, then yield control.
msg = pubsub.get_message(ignore_subscribe_messages=True, timeout=0.1)
⋮----
payload = json.loads(msg["data"])
⋮----
# Heartbeat keeps the connection alive through proxies / Cloudflare.
now = time.time()
⋮----
last_heartbeat = now
⋮----
# Yield control to the event loop so other requests aren't starved.
⋮----
def _format_sse(event: str, data: dict) -> str
⋮----
"""Format a single SSE frame."""
payload = json.dumps(data, default=str)
````

## File: midterm-dashboard/backend/historical_results.py
````python
"""Historical US election results.

Hand-curated dataset of recent federal/statewide election winners including
vote totals and margins. Used by the /data/historical endpoint so users can
compare current prediction markets against historical baselines.

Sources: state Secretaries of State, FEC, and Cook Political Report archives.
"""
⋮----
# Schema: (year, race_type, state, winner, party, winner_pct, runner_up,
#          runner_up_party, runner_up_pct, winner_votes, runner_up_votes, margin_pct)
⋮----
HISTORICAL_RESULTS = [
⋮----
# 2024 Presidential
⋮----
# 2024 Senate (key races)
⋮----
# 2022 Midterms — Senate
⋮----
# 2022 Midterms — Governor
⋮----
# 2020 Presidential
⋮----
# 2018 Midterms — key Senate races
⋮----
def get_results(year: int = None, race_type: str = None, state: str = None) -> list[dict]
⋮----
"""Filter historical results."""
results = HISTORICAL_RESULTS
⋮----
results = [r for r in results if r["year"] == year]
⋮----
results = [r for r in results if r["race_type"] == race_type]
⋮----
results = [r for r in results if r["state"].upper() == state.upper()]
````

## File: midterm-dashboard/backend/README.md
````markdown
# midterm-dashboard/backend/ — FastAPI server

The Python half of the Midterm Dashboard. FastAPI app that pulls election
predictions from Polymarket / Kalshi / PredictIt / polling aggregators,
stores them in SQLite, and exposes JSON endpoints + the built React frontend
on port 8051.

Run standalone:

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
python3 main.py
```

Or rely on the multi-stage `Dockerfile` in the parent directory, which builds
the frontend then runs this backend.

## Files in this directory

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `main.py` | The FastAPI app. Routes (`/data/*`, `/auth/*`, `/admin/*`), gateway SSO middleware, CORS for the Vite dev server, background data-refresh task (every 5 min), serves the built `frontend/dist/` as static files in production. Also exposes the **admin human-review** endpoints — `POST/DELETE /admin/race/{key}/flag` to mark a (source, source_id) pair as the wrong market for a race, and `POST/DELETE /admin/race/{key}/verify` to mark a race pairing as human-confirmed. `divergence_calculator`, `data_races`, and `data_race_detail` all consult `Database.get_all_wrong_flags()` once per request and skip flagged pairs so flagged matches disappear from listings and the divergence chart. |
| `database.py` | SQLite layer (WAL mode, threading lock, contextmanager). User auth was migrated out — the gateway handles users/sessions; this module only manages dashboard data and the shared `profiles` table. UUID string user IDs. Also owns the human-review tables `midterm_market_match_flags` (admin-flagged wrong markets, keyed by `(source, source_id, race_key)`) and `midterm_market_race_verifications` (admin-confirmed correct pairings, keyed by `race_key`), with `flag_market_as_wrong` / `unflag_market` / `get_flags_for_race` / `get_all_wrong_flags` / `verify_race` / `unverify_race` / `get_race_verification` / `get_all_verifications` upsert + lookup methods. |
| `district_profiles.py` | Hand-curated background context per state/district — demographics, economy, infrastructure, political history, geography. A background task in `main.py` keeps profiles fresh for every state with an active race. |
| `race_context.py` | Per-race context dictionary keyed by `{race_type}_{state}`: incumbents, likely candidates, state ballot measures, key issues, Cook/Sabato lean rating, narrative. Drives the "why is this market moving" view. |
| `historical_results.py` | Hand-curated dataset of recent federal/statewide election winners with vote totals and margins. Powers the `/data/historical` endpoint so users can compare current markets against historical baselines. |
| `test_race_key.py` | Standalone (no pytest) regression test for `market_race_key` — guards against the prior bug where unrelated "other/no-state" markets like "Bulgarian elections" and "LeBron James for president" collapsed into the same `other_US` bucket. Also covers the human-review DB round-trips: flag/unflag idempotency, verify/unverify upsert, and the divergence-calculator skip path. Run with `./venv/bin/python test_race_key.py`. |
| `requirements.txt` | Python deps (FastAPI, aiohttp, sqlmodel, etc.). Tiny — most logic is in stdlib + FastAPI. |
| `midterm_dashboard.db` | Main SQLite DB (~108MB, gitignored). Auto-created on first run. |

## Human-review of market matches

Even with the strict race-key matcher, the source aggregators occasionally
group the wrong Polymarket / Kalshi market into a race. The dashboard ships
an admin-only review loop:

1. An admin opens a race detail page and spots a source card that doesn't
   belong (e.g. a Polymarket "LeBron for president" market grouped under
   the Bulgarian parliamentary race).
2. They click **Wrong** on that source card. The frontend POSTs to
   `/admin/race/{race_key}/flag` with `{source, source_id, note?}`.
3. `Database.flag_market_as_wrong` upserts the row in
   `midterm_market_match_flags`.
4. On the next listing fetch, `data_races` and `divergence_calculator`
   skip the flagged pair, so it disappears from the dashboard everywhere.
5. The race detail endpoint `data_race_detail` keeps the flagged entry
   visible (with `flagged: true` and the admin's note) so the same admin
   can undo their own flag via the **Unflag** button.
6. Conversely, an admin can mark a known-good pairing as human-verified
   via `POST /admin/race/{key}/verify`. The frontend shows a "Verified"
   badge in the race header. This is informational only — verification
   doesn't change matching behaviour, it just records reviewer trust.

All admin endpoints sit behind `require_tier(request, "admin")`. Tests
live in `test_race_key.py`.

## Subdirectories

| Dir | Purpose | README |
|---|---|---|
| `aggregators/` | Source connectors for prediction markets and polling | `aggregators/README.md` |
| `templates/` | Reserved for future server-rendered templates (currently empty). | — |
````

````python
"""Tests for market_race_key.

Run with: ./venv/bin/python test_race_key.py
(no pytest dependency)

Guards the regression: prior to the fix, both Bulgarian elections and
"Will LeBron James be the next president" collapsed into the same
``other_US`` race_key bucket and the divergence calculator grouped them
as the same race across sources.
"""
⋮----
def assert_eq(actual, expected, label)
⋮----
def assert_ne(actual, other, label)
⋮----
def assert_starts(actual, prefix, label)
⋮----
# --- Real races still produce stable canonical keys -------------------------
⋮----
# Cross-source same race shares the key
poly_senate = {"race_type": "senate", "state": "GA", "source": "polymarket", "source_id": "p1"}
kalshi_senate = {"race_type": "senate", "state": "GA", "source": "kalshi", "source_id": "k1"}
⋮----
# House district parsing
⋮----
# House without parseable district → unmatched (do NOT collapse to state-level)
house_unparseable = market_race_key({
⋮----
# --- The regression: unrelated markets must NOT share a key -----------------
lebron = {
bulgaria = {
lebron_key = market_race_key(lebron)
bulgaria_key = market_race_key(bulgaria)
⋮----
# Even with the same source, source_id makes them distinct
poly_a = {"race_type": "other", "state": None, "source": "polymarket", "source_id": "a"}
poly_b = {"race_type": "other", "state": None, "source": "polymarket", "source_id": "b"}
⋮----
# Missing race_type entirely → unmatched
no_rt = {"state": "GA", "source": "polymarket", "source_id": "x"}
⋮----
# Missing state entirely → unmatched
no_state = {"race_type": "senate", "source": "polymarket", "source_id": "y"}
⋮----
# =============================================================================
# Human-review flag/verify round-trip tests
⋮----
# These exercise the real DB — we use a throwaway race_key/source_id prefix
# so we don't collide with any real data that may exist in data.db.
⋮----
_db = Database()
⋮----
_TEST_RACE = "test_race_key_review_xyz"
_TEST_SRC = "polymarket"
_TEST_ID = "test-market-review-xyz"
⋮----
# Clean slate in case a previous failed run left rows behind
⋮----
# Flag round-trip ------------------------------------------------------------
⋮----
flags = _db.get_flags_for_race(_TEST_RACE)
⋮----
all_flags = _db.get_all_wrong_flags()
⋮----
# Idempotency — flagging twice should not create duplicates
⋮----
flags2 = _db.get_flags_for_race(_TEST_RACE)
our_rows = [f for f in flags2 if f["source_id"] == _TEST_ID]
⋮----
# Unflag
removed = _db.unflag_market(_TEST_SRC, _TEST_ID, _TEST_RACE)
⋮----
flags3 = _db.get_flags_for_race(_TEST_RACE)
⋮----
# Unflagging a nonexistent row returns False (not an error)
⋮----
# Verify round-trip ----------------------------------------------------------
⋮----
v = _db.get_race_verification(_TEST_RACE)
⋮----
# Idempotent
⋮----
v2 = _db.get_race_verification(_TEST_RACE)
⋮----
# Unverify
removed_v = _db.unverify_race(_TEST_RACE)
⋮----
# Unverifying a nonexistent race returns False
⋮----
# Flag filtering simulates the divergence_calculator skip -------------------
# Add a flag, then check get_all_wrong_flags gives us the tuple we'd use
# in the real grouping loop.
⋮----
flags_map = _db.get_all_wrong_flags()
fake_market = {"source": _TEST_SRC, "source_id": _TEST_ID}
is_flagged = (fake_market["source"], fake_market["source_id"]) in flags_map.get(_TEST_RACE, set())
⋮----
# Cleanup
````

## File: midterm-dashboard/frontend/src/pages/README.md
````markdown
# midterm-dashboard/frontend/src/pages/ — Route components

One component per route. Mounted from `App.jsx`. Each page is self-contained
— it imports `api` from `../lib/api`, fetches what it needs in a `useEffect`,
and renders.

## Files in this directory

| File | Route | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `Dashboard.jsx` | `/` | Landing view — top movers, race summary cards, divergence highlights. |
| `Races.jsx` | `/races` | Filterable/sortable list of every Senate, House, and gubernatorial race. |
| `RaceDetail.jsx` | `/race/:id` | Single-race deep dive — current odds across markets, polling, candidates, history, divergence chart. **Admin-only human-review controls**: a "Verify match"/"Unverify" toggle in the race header (renders a green "Verified" badge when set), and a "Wrong"/"Unflag" toggle on each source card. Flagged source cards render grayed-out with strike-through and the reviewer's note. All four actions go through `api.flagMarket` / `api.unflagMarket` / `api.verifyRace` / `api.unverifyRace` and refetch the race after each call so the UI reflects the new state. |
| `Divergence.jsx` | `/divergence` | Cross-market divergence view — pairs of markets disagreeing about the same race. |
| `Historical.jsx` | `/historical` | Historical results comparison — current odds vs `historical_results.py` baselines. |
| `WorldElections.jsx` | `/world` | Global elections sidebar — non-US races tracked for context. |
| `AdminDashboard.jsx` | `/admin` | Admin-only panel — user management, manual data refresh, error logs. |
| `Account.jsx` | `/account` | Subscription / billing settings (the gateway handles real billing — this is the dashboard-side view). |
| `Settings.jsx` | `/settings` | User preferences (unit system, theme). Reads/writes via `useSettings()`. |
| `Login.jsx` | `/login` | Login form. POSTs to the gateway via `api.login()`. |
| `Register.jsx` | `/register` | Signup form. Disabled in production unless self-serve registration is enabled gateway-side. |
````

## File: midterm-dashboard/deploy.sh
````bash
#!/bin/bash
set -e

# MidtermEdge Deployment Script
# Deploys to Linux server, sets up Python venv, builds frontend, starts services

PROJECT_DIR="$HOME/midterm-dashboard"
BACKEND_DIR="$PROJECT_DIR/backend"
FRONTEND_DIR="$PROJECT_DIR/frontend"

echo "=== MidtermEdge Deployment ==="

# Ensure we're in the project directory
cd "$PROJECT_DIR"

# --- Python Backend Setup ---
echo "[1/5] Setting up Python environment..."
if [ ! -d "$BACKEND_DIR/venv" ]; then
    python3 -m venv "$BACKEND_DIR/venv"
fi
source "$BACKEND_DIR/venv/bin/activate"
pip install --quiet --upgrade pip
pip install --quiet -r "$BACKEND_DIR/requirements.txt"

# --- Frontend Build ---
echo "[2/5] Building frontend..."
cd "$FRONTEND_DIR"
if [ ! -d "node_modules" ]; then
    npm install
fi
npm run build
cd "$PROJECT_DIR"

# --- Create admin user if not exists ---
echo "[3/5] Initializing database..."
ADMIN_EMAIL="${ADMIN_EMAIL:-admin@midtermedge.com}"
ADMIN_PASSWORD="${ADMIN_PASSWORD:?Set ADMIN_PASSWORD before deploying}"
python3 -c "
import asyncio
import sys
import os
sys.path.insert(0, '$BACKEND_DIR')
from database import Database

async def init():
    email = os.environ['ADMIN_EMAIL']
    password = os.environ['ADMIN_PASSWORD']
    db = Database('$BACKEND_DIR/midterm_dashboard.db')
    await db.connect()
    # Create admin user if not exists
    existing = await db.get_user_by_email(email)
    if not existing:
        uid = await db.create_user(email, password, 'Admin')
        if uid:
            await db.update_user_tier(uid, 'admin')
            print(f'  Created admin user: {email}')
    else:
        print('  Admin user already exists')
    await db.close()

asyncio.run(init())
"

# --- Systemd Service ---
echo "[4/5] Setting up systemd service..."
SYSTEMD_DIR="$HOME/.config/systemd/user"
mkdir -p "$SYSTEMD_DIR"

cat > "$SYSTEMD_DIR/midtermedge.service" << EOF
[Unit]
Description=MidtermEdge Dashboard Server
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
WorkingDirectory=$BACKEND_DIR
ExecStart=$BACKEND_DIR/venv/bin/python main.py
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5
Environment=PORT=8050

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
EOF

systemctl --user daemon-reload
systemctl --user enable midtermedge.service
systemctl --user restart midtermedge.service

echo "[5/5] Service started!"
echo ""
echo "=== Deployment Complete ==="
echo "  Dashboard: http://$(hostname -I | awk '{print $1}'):8050"
echo "  Tailscale: http://$(tailscale ip -4 2>/dev/null || echo 'N/A'):8050"
echo "  Admin:     $ADMIN_EMAIL (password was set via \$ADMIN_PASSWORD)"
echo ""
echo "  Manage service:"
echo "    systemctl --user status midtermedge"
echo "    systemctl --user restart midtermedge"
echo "    journalctl --user -u midtermedge -f"
````

## File: polymarket_weather_bot/datastore.py
````python
"""SQLite datastore — log all signals and trades."""
⋮----
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
⋮----
class DataStore
⋮----
"""SQLite-backed storage for signals, trades, and PnL tracking."""
⋮----
def __init__(self, db_path: Optional[str] = None)
⋮----
def _init_db(self) -> None
⋮----
# Migrate existing databases: add platform column if missing
⋮----
def log_signal(self, signal: Signal) -> None
⋮----
now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
platform = getattr(signal.market, "platform", "polymarket")

side = "YES" if signal.action == "BUY_YES" else "NO"
price = signal.market_prob if side == "YES" else (1.0 - signal.market_prob)

⋮----
today = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
⋮----
signals = conn.execute(
trades = conn.execute(
actionable = conn.execute(
⋮----
def get_recent_trades(self, limit: int = 20) -> list
⋮----
rows = conn.execute(
⋮----

⋮----

⋮----
total = conn.execute(
by_action = conn.execute(

⋮----
# ── Calibration / Brier score ────────────────────────────────────────────
⋮----
def log_calibration(self, signal: Signal, outcome: int) -> None
⋮----
"""Record a resolved signal for calibration tracking.

        Args:
            signal: The original trading signal.
            outcome: 1 if YES resolved true, 0 if NO.
        """
⋮----
prob_method = getattr(signal, "prob_method", "gaussian")
⋮----
def get_brier_score(self) -> dict
⋮----
"""Compute Brier score and calibration stats from resolved signals.

        Brier score = mean((model_prob - outcome)^2).
        Lower is better; 0.25 = coin flip, 0.0 = perfect.

        Also computes the market's Brier score for comparison — if ours is
        lower, the model adds value over market prices.
        """
⋮----
n = len(rows)
brier_model = sum((r["model_prob"] - r["outcome"]) ** 2 for r in rows) / n
brier_market = sum((r["market_prob"] - r["outcome"]) ** 2 for r in rows) / n
⋮----
# Breakdown by method
by_method = {}
⋮----
method = r["prob_method"] or "gaussian"
⋮----
method_scores = {}
⋮----
# Breakdown by platform
by_platform = {}
⋮----
plat = r["platform"] or "polymarket"
⋮----
platform_scores = {}
⋮----
# Calibration buckets: group predictions into 10% bins
buckets = {}
⋮----
bucket = min(int(r["model_prob"] * 10), 9)  # 0-9
label = f"{bucket*10}-{bucket*10+10}%"
⋮----
calibration_buckets = {}
````
